title: Tetraphyle alpestris
intro: Tetraphyle alpestris, also known as Alpine St Johns Wort, is a perennial plant species in the family Hypericaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidepressant properties, making it a natural remedy for anxiety, insomnia, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tetraphyle alpestris include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alpine St Johns Wort is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to support mental health and well-being, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
